rules_evaluated.evidence: 5:You built a memory today. One live challenge, curated sources... — warm peer-to-peer Sutherland/Godin register throughout

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy, NOT in curriculum body
rules_evaluated.evidence: 81:A memory that sits there is a document. A memory that gets read by an agent... — metaphorical reframe, not defensive positioning

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: 17:Enable Google Calendar or Microsoft Outlook Calendar — context-earned

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: 87:One glance each morning for a week — 'morning' is time-of-day anchor
rules_evaluated.fix: Replace 'One glance each morning for a week' with 'One observation per run, over a week' or 'Watch the output for one week, one check per run'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: 27:Paste: — (semantic lead-in above fence); 41:Give Claude the URL... — (lead-in present); 61:Save. Click... — (procedural but covered)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block
rules_evaluated.evidence: 28:Prompt label missing destination chip: **Prompt** ← should be **Prompt** *(Claude Code)*; same at lines 43 and 63
rules_evaluated.fix: Add *(Claude Code)* parenthetical after each **Prompt** label (lines 29, 44, 64)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight
rules_evaluated.evidence: 30:**+** (bold asterisks in fence — renders literal); 31:**Settings → Connectors** (bold in fence); 45:**Schedule** (bold in fence); 49:**- Morning plan** / **- Daily risk** / **- Draft today's** (bold bullets inside fence at lines 46-48)
rules_evaluated.fix: Remove **bold** markup inside all three fenced blocks — use plain text or bullets only. Lines 30-31, 44-48: unmark everything from ``` to ```.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Question-dump default — in turn is too soft for one-at-a-time rhythm
rules_evaluated.evidence: 46-48:Three questions listed sequentially ('1. Which job', '2. What should', '3. What must'); prompt reads 'ask one at a time' nowhere, allowing Claude to batch-dump all three
rules_evaluated.fix: Inside the prompt at lines 46-51, add explicit one-at-a-time phrasing: 'Ask me one question, wait for my answer, then ask the next. Do not show me the question list.' Also remove the leading numbered list at lines 46-48 or keep it but add the one-at-a-time structural rule inside the fence.
